steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Use similarity to relate angles
steps.content: When two triangles are similar, their corresponding angles are equal. The problem states that angle $F$ corresponds to angle $J$. Therefore, angle $F = $ angle $J$.

steps.title: Apply the sine equality
steps.content:
Since angle $F = $ angle $J$, their sine values must be equal:
$$\sin(F) = \sin(J)$$
We are given that $\sin(F) = \frac{308}{317}$, so:
$$\sin(J) = \frac{308}{317}$$
That is the correct answer.
